snippets.title: Softmax from scratch (NumPy)
snippets.code:
import numpy as np

def softmax(z):
    z = np.asarray(z, dtype=np.float64)
    z_shift = z - z.max()  # numerical stability
    exp_z = np.exp(z_shift)
    return exp_z / exp_z.sum()

logits = np.array([2.0, 1.0, 0.5])
probs = softmax(logits)
print("pi:", probs)
print("sum:", probs.sum())
snippets.explanation: Subtracting max(z) before exp prevents overflow and does not change the result — the canonical softmax implementation pattern.

snippets.title: Log-sum-exp and log-softmax
snippets.explanation: Log-sum-exp computes log Σ exp(z_j) safely. log_softmax gives log π_k directly — preferred for cross-entropy loss.

snippets.title: Stable categorical cross-entropy on logits
snippets.code:
import numpy as np

def cross_entropy(logits, y_true):
    log_pi = logits - log_sum_exp(logits)
    return -log_pi[y_true]

logits = np.array([2.0, 1.0, 0.5])
print("loss class 0:", cross_entropy(logits, 0))
print("loss class 1:", cross_entropy(logits, 1))
snippets.explanation: Loss −log π_y computed via log_softmax avoids computing tiny probabilities then taking log.

snippets.title: PyTorch: logits in, loss out
snippets.code:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F

logits = torch.tensor([[2.0, 1.0, 0.5]])
target = torch.tensor([0])

loss_fn = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
print("CrossEntropyLoss:", loss_fn(logits, target).item())

log_probs = F.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1)
print("manual -log pi_y:", (-log_probs[0, target]).item())
print("softmax pi:", F.softmax(logits, dim=-1).tolist())
snippets.explanation: Pass raw logits to CrossEntropyLoss; it applies log_softmax internally. Use F.softmax only when you need probabilities for inference or metrics.
